# UC-22.2.6 · NIS2 Risk Analysis and Information System Security Policy Evidence

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Risk, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep an eye on your risk register and official security rulebooks so overdue reviews and half-finished approvals show up in one list. That way the people responsible can fix paperwork before it becomes a crisis.*

---

## Description

Joins normalized risk-register events and ServiceNow policy rows to a static policy and cadence table, so open high scores past due reviews, stale approvals, and non-approved policy drafts appear as one auditor-facing queue.

## Value

Risk and policy teams spend less time reconciling three spreadsheets before board meetings because the same search proves reviews ran, documents who owns each line, and shows where acceptance or republishing slipped.

## Implementation

(1) Land `grc:risk` via TA pull or HEC with stable `risk_id`. (2) Enable `snow:sn_grc_policy` input with RBAC scoped to the information security policy catalog. (3) Maintain `nis2_is_security_policies.csv` from your GRC tool of record. (4) Schedule weekly; route to the risk committee digest channel. (5) Sign exports per `evidenceSigning`.

## Evidence

Scheduled `UC-22.2.6` compliance report, signed CSV/PDF bundle, and chain-of-custody log referencing the RFC3161 token for each quarterly submission to the board pack.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Seed `index=grc` with `risk_id=RISK-9001`, `risk_score=80`, `risk_status=open`, `next_review_date` set three months in the past, and confirm the Monday schedule emails a row with `source_lane=risk_register`; seed `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` with `approval_status=draft` for `policy_number=POL-5001` and verify it appears with `source_lane=policy_record` while approved policies with fresh `last_approved` remain absent.

### Negative scenario

Move every in-scope risk to `risk_status=treated` with a future `next_review_date`, flip all policies to `approval_status=approved` inside the CSV `review_cycle_days` window, reload the knowledge bundle, and rerun ad-hoc to get zero rows even though historical drill data still exists in the indexes for traceability.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
- **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
- **Indexes**: `grc`, `itsm` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
- **Lookups**: `nis2_is_security_policies` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_is_security_policies')`.
- **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.21(2)(a)`. The control objective is: "Links live GRC risk-register rows and infosec policy approvals to `nis2_is_security_policies` so overdue reviews, high open scores, and stale approvals surface in one queue." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `grc`, `itsm` under `grc:risk`, `snow:sn_grc_policy`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=grc earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


- **Hint 1:** Land `grc:risk` via TA pull or HEC with stable `risk_id`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

- **Hint 2:** Enable `snow:sn_grc_policy` input with RBAC scoped to the information security policy catalog. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

- **Hint 3:** Maintain `nis2_is_security_policies.csv` from your GRC tool of record. The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

- **Hint 4:** Schedule weekly; route to the risk committee digest channel. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

- **Hint 5:** Sign exports per `evidenceSigning`. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Risk Analysis and Information System Security Policy Evidence," the aggregation is the evidence artifact itself — auditors will read these rows as the official record of NIS2 control activity.

This detection correlates event arrival time with a business timestamp from `last_approved` (format: `%Y-%m-%d`). The `strptime()` conversion to epoch is the linchpin — the subsequent `where` clause compares Splunk's `_time` against the parsed epoch to detect policy violations (activity after a termination, events past a retention boundary, operations outside a maintenance window, etc).

Silent failure mode: if `last_approved` arrives in a format the `strptime` call doesn't match, the conversion returns null, propagates through the `where` comparison as 'unknown,' and the row is dropped from results. This produces under-reporting, not over-reporting — which is the dangerous direction for a compliance detection. Validate by running:

```spl
<base search>
| eval last_approved_epoch = strptime(last_approved, "%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(last_approved_epoch)
| stats count by last_approved
```

If this returns rows, the upstream system has multiple timestamp formats (common with SaaS connectors that change during version upgrades) and the `strptime` call must be hardened with `coalesce(strptime(last_approved, "fmt1"), strptime(last_approved, "fmt2"))` to handle them all.

Time-zone alignment: if the source system emits `last_approved` in a different zone from the Splunk search head, the comparison will be offset. Look for `%z` or `%Z` in the format string — if absent, the conversion uses `TZ = TZ of Splunk host`, which is correct only when source and target agree. Search covers the last 730 days.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.6: NIS2 Risk Analysis and Information System Security Policy Ev]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -730d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.6,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `approval_status`, `days_since_approved`, `last_approved`, `next_review_date`, `policy_name`, `record_key`, `review_breach`, `review_cycle_days`, `risk_score`, `risk_status`. Run:

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table approval_status, days_since_approved, last_approved, next_review_date, policy_name, record_key, review_breach, review_cycle_days
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `nis2_is_security_policies`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_is_security_policies
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(d) Threshold calibration:** Before enabling as a production alert, run the base search with the threshold removed and profile the distribution of `risk_score`:

```spl
<base search> earliest=-30d
| stats perc50(risk_score) as p50 perc90(risk_score) as p90 perc99(risk_score) as p99 max(risk_score) as hi
```

The current threshold `75` should sit between p90 and p99 for a balanced signal-to-noise ratio. If it's below p50, expect heavy alert volume; if above p99, expect under-detection.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.6: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Dashboard Studio: Matrix By Source Lane; Bar Chart Of Aging `Days Since Approved`; Risk Score Trend Via Overlay `Timechart` On `Index=Grc` Subset Only (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**Silent under-reporting:** The `strptime` conversion is likely failing on a subset of events — see Step 3 for the hardening procedure. Check for format drift after upstream system upgrades.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

- 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

- Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

- Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

- Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.

## SPL

```spl
(index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk earliest=-730d@d) OR (index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-730d@d)
| eval record_key=coalesce(risk_id,policy_number,u_policy_id)
| eval source_lane=if(index="grc","risk_register","policy_record")
| lookup nis2_is_security_policies policy_id AS record_key OUTPUT policy_name, approval_status, last_approved, review_cycle_days
| eval approved_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_approved),strptime(last_approved,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval days_since_approval=if(isnotnull(approved_epoch),round((now()-approved_epoch)/86400,1),null())
| eval next_epoch=if(isnotnull(next_review_date),strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval review_breach=0
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="risk_register" AND isnotnull(next_epoch) AND now()>next_epoch,1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="risk_register" AND risk_score>=75 AND match(lower(risk_status),"open|new|assessing"),1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="policy_record" AND match(lower(approval_status),"draft|pending|rejected"),1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="policy_record" AND isnotnull(days_since_approval) AND days_since_approval>review_cycle_days,1,review_breach)
| where review_breach=1
| stats latest(risk_score) as risk_score latest(approval_status) as approval_status latest(days_since_approval) as days_since_approved latest(policy_name) as policy_name by record_key source_lane
| sort 0 - risk_score
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio: matrix by source_lane; bar chart of aging `days_since_approved`; risk score trend via overlay `timechart` on `index=grc` subset only.

## Known False Positives

**Quarterly risk workshop holds scores open on purpose:** Distinguish tickets tagged `workshop_hold` in the source system. Suppress with `lookup nis2_risk_exception_register risk_id OUTPUT suppress_until | where now()>suppress_until OR isnull(suppress_until)`.

**Policy translations pending legal review:** Distinguish `approval_status=pending` with `u_locale` not equal to master language. Suppress via `where NOT (approval_status="pending" AND u_locale!="en")` until legal signs off.

**ServiceNow clone noise:** Distinguish `sys_created_by=clone` or `instance=dev`. Suppress using `where instance="prod"` after indexing `instance` from the TA configuration.

**Lookup key drift after CMDB merge:** Distinguish blank `record_key` after merge events. Suppress nothing; fix CSV keys and use the exception register until remapped.

**Duplicate backlog replays:** Distinguish `_cd` duplication during indexer recovery. Suppress with `| dedup record_key source_lane` in the alert-only variant.
